chapters.title: A Sunday lunch in Ikoyi
chapters.body: Eni arrived late, in a yellow dress. Tiwa abandoned his conversation in the middle of a sentence. The friend who introduced us has never let us forget it.

chapters.title: The lockdown letters
chapters.body: Two cities apart, we wrote properly, by paper and by post, for ninety two days. The handwriting got softer.

chapters.title: Christmas in Abeokuta
chapters.body: Eni met the Ọmọ́tọ̀shọ́s. Mama said little; she watched a great deal. By Boxing Day she had named the future grandchildren.

chapters.title: The proposal
chapters.body: On the balcony at Cap Manuel, just after the call to prayer. He had practised the speech for weeks. He said three words.

chapters.title: The wedding day
chapters.body: And now, at last, you. With us. In white. Singing.
